title: Spicy Tender Monsoon Vegetables Stir-fry
description: A spicy vegetarian dish inspired by Arabian cuisine, featuring sweetened condensed milk for a unique sweetness.

ingredients.name: Bell peppers
ingredients.notes: Remove seeds and membranes for a milder taste.

ingredients.name: Chili peppers
ingredients.notes: Can be substituted with red pepper flakes for a milder option.

ingredients.name: Sweetened condensed milk
ingredients.notes: Use unsweetened if preferred or available, otherwise adjust sweetness accordingly.

instructions: Wash and chop all vegetables into uniform sizes.
In a small bowl, mix the sweetened condensed milk with enough water to reach about 1/4 cup of liquid. Set aside.
Heat oil in a pressure cooker or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant but not browned, about 30 seconds.
Add chopped vegetables and chili peppers. Stir-fry for about 5 minutes or until the vegetables start to soften.
Pour in the sweetened condensed milk mixture and add turmeric powder. Mix well to coat all vegetables evenly.
Cover and cook on high pressure for about 1 minute if using a pressure cooker, or stir occasionally over medium heat until fully cooked through, about 5-7 minutes more.
If not using a pressure cooker, continue cooking the dish without covering, ensuring it does not stick to the pan by stirring frequently and adding a bit of water if needed.
Serve hot, garnished with sesame seeds if desired.
